title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
summary: Best AI engineers use automated loops to prompt agents repeatedly rather than prompting manually. These loops can orchestrate multiple worker agents in parallel, with an orchestrator deciding tasks. The approach reduces manual intervention but increases token costs and requires reliable observability harnesses.
writeup: Cole Medin examines a shift in how leading AI engineers work with coding agents: instead of prompting manually, they build automated loops that prompt agents repeatedly. The video describes orchestration patterns where an orchestrator agent assigns tasks and coordinates multiple worker agents running in parallel. The tradeoffs are concrete: less manual intervention at the cost of higher token consumption and a dependence on reliable observability harnesses to track agent behavior.
